name: execute_query
description: Execute a SQL query against an InfluxDB database (all versions). Returns results in the specified format (defaults to JSON). Large Dataset Warning: InfluxDB might contain massive time-series data. Always use COUNT(*) first to check size, then LIMIT/OFFSET for large results (>1000 rows). Cloud Dedicated/Clustered & Cloud Serverless (v3) Requirements: GROUP BY: Include all group columns in SELECT; Aggregations: Cast and alias COUNT (e.g., CAST(COUNT(*) AS DOUBLE) AS count).

database.description: Name of the database/bucket to query

name: get_measurements
description: Get a list of all measurements (tables) in a database/bucket (all versions). Uses the InfluxDB information_schema.columns to discover tables.

database.description: Name of the database/bucket to list measurements from

name: get_measurement_schema
description: Get the schema (column information) for a specific measurement/table (all versions). Shows column names, types, and categories (time/tag/field).

database.description: Name of the database/bucket containing the measurement

measurement.description: Name of the measurement to describe

name: write_line_protocol
description: Write data to InfluxDB using line protocol format (all versions). Supports single records or batches. Line Protocol Syntax: measurement,tag1=value1,tag2=value2 field1=value1,field2=value2 timestamp. Field Value Types: Strings: "quoted string"; Floats: 123.45; Integers: 123i (note the 'i' suffix); Booleans: t or f. Always specify correct precision parameter to match your timestamp format.

database.description: Name of the database/bucket to write to

data.description: Line protocol formatted data. For multiple records, separate each line with \n

noSync.description: Acknowledge without waiting for WAL persistence

name: create_database
description: Create a new database in InfluxDB. Database names must follow InfluxDB naming rules: alphanumeric characters, dashes (-), underscores (_), and forward slashes (/) are allowed. Must start with a letter or number. Maximum 64 characters. For Cloud Dedicated/Clustered: maxTables, maxColumnsPerTable, retentionPeriod. For Cloud Serverless: description, retentionPeriod.

name.description: Name of the database to create (alphanumeric, -, _, / allowed; max 64 chars; must start with letter/number)

description.description: Description for the bucket/database (Cloud Serverless only)

maxTables.description: Maximum number of tables (Cloud Dedicated/Clustered only, default: 500)

maxColumnsPerTable.description: Maximum columns per table (Cloud Dedicated/Clustered only, default: 200)

retentionPeriod.description: Retention period in nanoseconds (Cloud Dedicated/Clustered: default 0 = no expiration, Cloud Serverless: default 30 days)

name: serverless_list_schemas
description: List all measurement schemas in a Cloud Serverless bucket. Only available for buckets with explicit schema type. Shows schema names, column definitions, and metadata.

bucketName.description: Name of the Cloud Serverless bucket

name: serverless_get_schema
description: Get detailed information about a specific measurement schema in a Cloud Serverless bucket. Shows column definitions, data types, and schema metadata.

bucketName.description: Name of the Cloud Serverless bucket

schemaName.description: Name of the schema to retrieve

name: serverless_create_schema
description: Create a new measurement schema in a Cloud Serverless bucket with explicit schema type. Define columns with their types (tag, field, timestamp) and data types.

bucketName.description: Name of the Cloud Serverless bucket

schemaName.description: Name of the schema to create

name: serverless_update_schema
description: Add new columns to an existing measurement schema in a Cloud Serverless bucket. IMPORTANT: You can only ADD new columns, not modify existing ones. The endpoint requires ALL columns (existing + new) to be sent. Use get_schema first to retrieve current columns, then include them along with new columns in this update.

bucketName.description: Name of the Cloud Serverless bucket

schemaName.description: Name of the schema to update

columns.description: Array of ALL columns (existing + new). Must include all existing columns.

name: cloud_list_database_tokens
description: List all database tokens for InfluxDB Cloud-Dedicated/Clustered cluster. Returns token information including permissions and creation dates.

name: cloud_get_database_token
description: Get details of a specific database token by ID for InfluxDB Cloud-Dedicated/Clustered cluster.

tokenId.description: ID of the database token to retrieve

name: cloud_create_database_token
description: Create a new database token for InfluxDB Cloud-Dedicated/Clustered cluster. Specify exact permissions per database or create a no-access token. Permissions format: [{"database": "db_name", "action": "read|write"}, ...]

permissions.description: Array of permission objects. Each has database and action (read|write). Use [] for no-access token.

database.description: Database name, use * for all databases

name: cloud_update_database_token
description: Update an existing database token for InfluxDB Cloud-Dedicated/Clustered cluster. Can update description and/or permissions. Note: Permissions completely replace existing ones - include all desired permissions.

tokenId.description: ID of the token to update

permissions.description: New permissions (replaces all existing). Each has database and action (read|write).

name: cloud_delete_database_token
description: Delete a database token from InfluxDB Cloud-Dedicated/Clustered cluster. This action cannot be undone.

tokenId.description: ID of the token to delete

name: load_database_context
description: Check for and load custom database context if available. Always check this first as it can significantly speed up analysis and clarify data nuances. User-provided context is optional but when present, it contains valuable information about database structure, business context, or personal notes.

name: get_help
description: Get help and troubleshooting guidance for InfluxDB operations. Supports specific categories or keyword search.
